# Data-Visualization-project

[Pierce_Unsupervised Learning.ipynb](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27092350/Pierce_Unsupervised.Learning.ipynb)
{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "d1ac795b-b77d-4dd9-95db-4cc924d6a1e1",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Dataset Preview:\n",
      "   CustomerID  Gender  Age      Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "0           1    Male   19  15,000 USD             39\n",
      "1           2    Male   21  15,000 USD             81\n",
      "2           3  Female   20  16,000 USD              6\n",
      "3           4  Female   23  16,000 USD             77\n",
      "4           5  Female   31  17,000 USD             40\n",
      "\n",
      "Data Types:\n",
      "CustomerID        int64\n",
      "Gender           object\n",
      "Age               int64\n",
      "Income           object\n",
      "SpendingScore     int64\n",
      "dtype: object\n",
      "\n",
      "Missing Values:\n",
      "CustomerID       0\n",
      "Gender           0\n",
      "Age              0\n",
      "Income           0\n",
      "SpendingScore    0\n",
      "dtype: int64\n",
      "\n",
      "After Conversion - Data Types:\n",
      "CustomerID        int64\n",
      "Gender           object\n",
      "Age               int64\n",
      "Income            int64\n",
      "SpendingScore     int64\n",
      "dtype: object\n",
      "\n",
      "Dataset After Conversion:\n",
      "   CustomerID  Gender  Age  Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "0           1    Male   19   15000             39\n",
      "1           2    Male   21   15000             81\n",
      "2           3  Female   20   16000              6\n",
      "3           4  Female   23   16000             77\n",
      "4           5  Female   31   17000             40\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "# Load the dataset\n",
    "mallcustomers = pd.read_csv('mallcustomers.csv')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Preview the dataset\n",
    "print(\"Dataset Preview:\")\n",
    "print(mallcustomers.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check data types\n",
    "print(\"\\nData Types:\")\n",
    "print(mallcustomers.dtypes)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check for missing values\n",
    "print(\"\\nMissing Values:\")\n",
    "print(mallcustomers.isnull().sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Convert Income from string to numeric\n",
    "# First, remove \",\" and \"USD\" from the Income column\n",
    "mallcustomers['Income'] = mallcustomers['Income'].str.replace(',', '').str.replace('USD', '').str.strip()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Convert to numeric\n",
    "mallcustomers['Income'] = pd.to_numeric(mallcustomers['Income'])\n",
    "\n",
    "# Verify the conversion\n",
    "print(\"\\nAfter Conversion - Data Types:\")\n",
    "print(mallcustomers.dtypes)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Preview the dataset after conversion\n",
    "print(\"\\nDataset After Conversion:\")\n",
    "print(mallcustomers.head())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "a5952db5-8a49-41ed-b207-cbdb59c2365d",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Data for Clustering:\n",
      "   Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "0   15000             39\n",
      "1   15000             81\n",
      "2   16000              6\n",
      "3   16000             77\n",
      "4   17000             40\n",
      "\n",
      "Data Types:\n",
      "Income           int64\n",
      "SpendingScore    int64\n",
      "dtype: object\n",
      "\n",
      "Basic Statistics:\n",
      "              Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "count     200.000000     200.000000\n",
      "mean    60560.000000      50.200000\n",
      "std     26264.721165      25.823522\n",
      "min     15000.000000       1.000000\n",
      "25%     41500.000000      34.750000\n",
      "50%     61500.000000      50.000000\n",
      "75%     78000.000000      73.000000\n",
      "max    137000.000000      99.000000\n",
      "\n",
      "Missing Values:\n",
      "Income           0\n",
      "SpendingScore    0\n",
      "dtype: int64\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Convert Income from string to numeric - with error handling\n",
    "if mallcustomers['Income'].dtype == 'object':  # Check if the column contains strings\n",
    "    # Apply string operations only if the column contains strings\n",
    "    mallcustomers['Income'] = mallcustomers['Income'].str.replace(',', '').str.replace('USD', '').str.strip()\n",
    "    mallcustomers['Income'] = pd.to_numeric(mallcustomers['Income'])\n",
    "elif not pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(mallcustomers['Income']):\n",
    "    # If it's not string but also not numeric, try direct conversion\n",
    "    mallcustomers['Income'] = pd.to_numeric(mallcustomers['Income'], errors='coerce')\n",
    "# If it's already numeric, no conversion needed\n",
    "\n",
    "# Exclude CustomerID and keep only Income and SpendingScore\n",
    "clustering_data = mallcustomers[['Income', 'SpendingScore']]\n",
    "\n",
    "# Preview the data that will be used for clustering\n",
    "print(\"Data for Clustering:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check data types to ensure both columns are numeric\n",
    "print(\"\\nData Types:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.dtypes)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Basic statistics of the clustering variables\n",
    "print(\"\\nBasic Statistics:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.describe())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check for any missing values\n",
    "print(\"\\nMissing Values:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.isnull().sum())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "6433d37c-5322-4006-bf07-03040d073333",
   "metadata": {
    "editable": true,
    "slideshow": {
     "slide_type": ""
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Summary Statistics:\n",
      "              Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "count     200.000000     200.000000\n",
      "mean    60560.000000      50.200000\n",
      "std     26264.721165      25.823522\n",
      "min     15000.000000       1.000000\n",
      "25%     41500.000000      34.750000\n",
      "50%     61500.000000      50.000000\n",
      "75%     78000.000000      73.000000\n",
      "max    137000.000000      99.000000\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x500 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "\n",
      "Summary Statistics After Z-Score Normalization:\n",
      "             Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "count  2.000000e+02   2.000000e+02\n",
      "mean  -1.421085e-16  -1.465494e-16\n",
      "std    1.002509e+00   1.002509e+00\n",
      "min   -1.738999e+00  -1.910021e+00\n",
      "25%   -7.275093e-01  -5.997931e-01\n",
      "50%    3.587926e-02  -7.764312e-03\n",
      "75%    6.656748e-01   8.851316e-01\n",
      "max    2.917671e+00   1.894492e+00\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x500 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "\n",
      "Original Data - First 5 rows:\n",
      "   Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "0   15000             39\n",
      "1   15000             81\n",
      "2   16000              6\n",
      "3   16000             77\n",
      "4   17000             40\n",
      "\n",
      "Normalized Data - First 5 rows:\n",
      "     Income  SpendingScore\n",
      "0 -1.738999      -0.434801\n",
      "1 -1.738999       1.195704\n",
      "2 -1.700830      -1.715913\n",
      "3 -1.700830       1.040418\n",
      "4 -1.662660      -0.395980\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Display summary statistics\n",
    "print(\"Summary Statistics:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.describe())\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
    "\n",
    "# Visualize the distribution of features before normalization\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)\n",
    "plt.boxplot(clustering_data['Income'])\n",
    "plt.title('Income Distribution')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)\n",
    "plt.boxplot(clustering_data['SpendingScore'])\n",
    "plt.title('Spending Score Distribution')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Perform z-score normalization (standardization)\n",
    "scaler = StandardScaler()\n",
    "normalized_data = scaler.fit_transform(clustering_data)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Convert back to DataFrame for easier interpretation\n",
    "normalized_df = pd.DataFrame(normalized_data, columns=['Income', 'SpendingScore'])\n",
    "\n",
    "# Display summary statistics after normalization\n",
    "print(\"\\nSummary Statistics After Z-Score Normalization:\")\n",
    "print(normalized_df.describe())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Visualize the distribution of features after normalization\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)\n",
    "plt.boxplot(normalized_df['Income'])\n",
    "plt.title('Normalized Income Distribution')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)\n",
    "plt.boxplot(normalized_df['SpendingScore'])\n",
    "plt.title('Normalized Spending Score Distribution')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Compare original and normalized data\n",
    "print(\"\\nOriginal Data - First 5 rows:\")\n",
    "print(clustering_data.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\nNormalized Data - First 5 rows:\")\n",
    "print(normalized_df.head())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "ba0616a9-fd60-496b-98b3-125658143e23",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stderr",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n",
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from sklearn.cluster import KMeans\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate WCSS for different k values\n",
    "wcss = []\n",
    "for i in range(1, 11):\n",
    "    # Set random_state=42 for reproducibility\n",
    "    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=i, init='k-means++', random_state=42)\n",
    "    kmeans.fit(normalized_data)\n",
    "    wcss.append(kmeans.inertia_)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Plot the Elbow Curve\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))\n",
    "plt.plot(range(1, 11), wcss, marker='o', linestyle='--')\n",
    "plt.title('Elbow Method for Optimal K')\n",
    "plt.xlabel('Number of Clusters')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('WCSS')\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "2238824a-2d5f-4c6a-96f6-9793832eecef",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stderr",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "C:\\Users\\kpier\\anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\sklearn\\cluster\\_kmeans.py:1429: UserWarning: KMeans is known to have a memory leak on Windows with MKL, when there are less chunks than available threads. You can avoid it by setting the environment variable OMP_NUM_THREADS=1.\n",
      "  warnings.warn(\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x700 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Assuming optimal k is 5 based on the plot\n",
    "kmeans_final = KMeans(n_clusters=5, init='k-means++', random_state=42)\n",
    "y_kmeans = kmeans_final.fit_predict(normalized_data)\n",
    "\n",
    "mallcustomers = pd.read_csv('mallcustomers.csv')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Add cluster labels to the original dataframe for demographic analysis\n",
    "mallcustomers['Cluster'] = y_kmeans\n",
    "\n",
    "# Visualization (Assuming columns are Annual Income and Spending Score)\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))\n",
    "colors = ['red', 'blue', 'green', 'cyan', 'magenta']\n",
    "for i in range(5):\n",
    "    plt.scatter(mallcustomers[mallcustomers['Cluster'] == i]['Income'], \n",
    "                mallcustomers[mallcustomers['Cluster'] == i]['SpendingScore'], \n",
    "                s=100, c=colors[i], label=f'Cluster {i}')\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.title('Mall Customer Segments')\n",
    "plt.xlabel('Income')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('SpendingScore')\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "70de6d0c-26e5-4b9a-9d2d-16539158ca46",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Based on the typical visualization of Mall Customer data (Income vs. Spending), here are the standard names and the code to extract requested demographic info:\n",
    "# Cluster 0: \"Sensible\" (Mid income, Mid spending)\n",
    "# Cluster 1: \"Careless\" (Low income, High spending)\n",
    "# Cluster 2: \"Target\" (High income, High spending)\n",
    "# Cluster 3: \"Frugal\" (High income, Low spending)\n",
    "# Cluster 4: \"Miser\" (Low income, Low spending)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "3ff91564-7baf-405f-9222-59a20bc744b5",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "           Mean Age                                Gender Distribution\n",
      "Cluster                                                               \n",
      "Sensible  42.716049  {'Female': 0.5925925925925926, 'Male': 0.40740...\n",
      "Careless  32.692308  {'Female': 0.5384615384615384, 'Male': 0.46153...\n",
      "Target    25.272727  {'Female': 0.5909090909090909, 'Male': 0.40909...\n",
      "Frugal    41.114286  {'Male': 0.5428571428571428, 'Female': 0.45714...\n",
      "Miser     45.217391  {'Female': 0.6086956521739131, 'Male': 0.39130...\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Calculate Demographic Statistics\n",
    "demographics = mallcustomers.groupby('Cluster').agg({\n",
    "    'Age': 'mean',\n",
    "    'Gender': lambda x: x.value_counts(normalize=True).to_dict()\n",
    "}).rename(columns={'Age': 'Mean Age', 'Gender': 'Gender Distribution'})\n",
    "\n",
    "# Map the visual names for clarity\n",
    "cluster_names = {0: 'Sensible', 1: 'Careless', 2: 'Target', 3: 'Frugal', 4: 'Miser'}\n",
    "demographics.index = demographics.index.map(cluster_names)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(demographics)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "09d91ddd-c0de-43c5-90b4-3ed703a4aae9",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "2c7bcc24-d289-4221-bed0-457451fe0476",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "1d1db4a7-b4d2-4bce-87ad-60217a7a7b8e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "992ce9a1-d0c8-4744-a30e-61d5c12f8fec",

  }
 ],
